name: Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove
description: A UNESCO-listed sacred forest with shrines, sculptures, and the mystical Osun River. The annual Osun-Osogbo Festival (August) attracts thousands.

name: Olumirin Waterfall (Erin-Ijesha Waterfalls)
description: A stunning 7-tier waterfall with natural pools. Visitors can climb steps to different levels for breathtaking views.

name: Nike Art Gallery
description: A vibrant gallery showcasing Nigerian art, including Adire textiles, paintings, and sculptures by Nike Davies-Okundaye.

name: Ooni's Palace (Ile-Ife)
description: The palace of the Ooni of Ife, spiritual head of the Yoruba people. Features ancient artifacts and royal history.

name: Ife Museum & Natural History Museum
description: Houses ancient Ife bronzes, terracotta works, and artifacts from the Yoruba kingdom.

name: Ayikunugba Waterfall
description: A serene, hidden waterfall with a cave and natural pool—great for picnics. Ayikunugba offers a refreshing escape for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike.

name: Mbari-Mbayo Cultural Centre
description: A hub for Yoruba art, drumming, and dance performances. Founded by artist Susanne Wenger.

name: Osun-Osogbo Festival
description: A 2-week festival with drumming, masquerades, and a procession to the Osun River (held in August).

name: Olojo Festival
description: Celebrates the creation of the world. The Ooni wears the sacred 'Ade Aare' crown and leads rituals (held in October).

name: Adire Shopping at Oja Oba Market
description: Buy hand-dyed Adire textiles, beads, and crafts from local artisans. Shoppers can explore colorful stalls filled with intricate designs, interact with local artisans, and even witness the dyeing process firsthand.
